title: chenopodium album
intro: Chenopodium album, commonly known as lamb's quarters, is a type of annual plant that belongs to the Amaranthaceae family. It has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of lamb's quarters include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting the immune system, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and anticancer properties. In herbal preparations, lamb's quarters can be consumed as a tea, made by infusing the leaves in hot water, or used in tincture form to extract its medicinal properties.
